response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, I would suggest several strategies. Firstly, it is essential to be aware of the sources of fake news and propaganda. This can be achieved by following reputable news sources and fact-checking websites. Secondly, it is crucial to be cautious when reading or sharing content on social media platforms. If a post looks too good to be true or seems suspicious, do not share it or believe it without verifying its authenticity. Thirdly, social media platforms must take responsibility for removing fake content from their platforms and imposing penalties on those who create and share such content. Finally, educating the public about the dangers of fake news and propaganda can help prevent its spread. This can be achieved through school curricula or community-based initiatives.",
"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be aware of the sources of fake news and propaganda, be cautious when reading or sharing content, hold social media platforms accountable for removing fake content, and educate the public about the dangers of fake news.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, I would suggest several strategies. Firstly, it is essential to be aware of the sources of fake news and propaganda. This can be achieved by following reputable news sources and fact-checking websites. Secondly, it is crucial to be cautious when reading or sharing content on social media platforms. If a post looks too good to be true or seems suspicious, do not share it or believe it without verifying its authenticity. Thirdly, social media platforms must take responsibility for removing fake content from their platforms and imposing penalties on those who create and share such content. Finally, educating the public about the dangers of fake news and propaganda can help prevent its spread. This can be achieved through school curricula or community-based initiatives.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to be aware of the sources of fake news and propaganda, be cautious when reading or sharing content, hold social media platforms accountable for removing fake content, and educate the public about the dangers of fake news.
